name: BB84 Protocol Link
description: Quantum Key Distribution using BB84 protocol with single-photon transmission over fiber or free-space channels for unconditional information-theoretic security.

name: E91 Protocol Link
description: Quantum Key Distribution based on Bell inequality violation using entangled photon pairs for end-to-end eavesdropping detection.

name: CV-QKD (Continuous Variable)
description: Continuous variable quantum key distribution using entangled light states measured via homodyne detection, enabling high-speed practical QKD.

name: DV-QKD (Discrete Variable)
description: Discrete variable quantum key distribution using single photons or entangled photon pairs with discrete measurement outcomes for secure key generation.

name: Quantum Repeater Network
description: Multi-hop quantum network using repeater nodes to extend QKD range beyond single-fiber limitations through entanglement swapping.

name: Quantum Internet Testbed
description: Experimental quantum internet ecosystem combining multiple QKD methods with quantum memory and repeater nodes for practical quantum networking.

name: Entanglement Distribution Network
description: Quantum network distributing entangled photon pairs across multiple nodes for quantum teleportation and distributed quantum computing.

name: Quantum Memory Network
description: Network of quantum memory nodes storing and retrieving entangled states to extend quantum communication range and enable quantum repeater operation.

name: Quantum Satellite Link (Micius)
description: Free-space quantum channel using Micius satellite for ground-to-orbit single-photon transmission enabling global-scale quantum key distribution.

name: Free-Space Quantum Channel
description: Quantum communication over line-of-sight free-space path using single photons or entangled states, subject to atmospheric turbulence effects.

name: Fiber Quantum Channel
description: Quantum communication over standard or specialized optical fiber with quantum repeaters for extended range secure key distribution.

name: Quantum Random Number Generator Network
description: Distributed network of quantum random number generators providing genuine entropy sources to multiple nodes over network interface.

name: Post-Quantum VPN Tunnel
description: Virtual private network using post-quantum cryptographic algorithms resistant to quantum computer attacks for future-proof secure communication.

name: Quantum-Safe Mesh Network
description: Mesh topology network using post-quantum algorithms for node-to-node encryption with distributed key management and self-healing.

name: Hybrid Classical-Quantum Network
description: Integrated network architecture combining classical IP backbone with quantum key distribution and post-quantum encryption layers.

name: AFDX (Avionics Full-Duplex Switched Ethernet)
description: Deterministic switched Ethernet architecture for avionics with guaranteed bandwidth, low latency, and fault tolerance meeting ARINC 664.

name: ARINC 429
description: Legacy unidirectional avionics data bus standard using label-based message format for point-to-point communication between avionics components.

name: ARINC 629
description: Bidirectional multi-transmitter avionics bus supporting higher bandwidth than ARINC 429 with distributed clock synchronization.

name: ARINC 818 (Avionics Digital Video Bus)
description: High-speed avionics digital video interface standard for cockpit displays and cabin entertainment systems with point-to-point topology.

name: MIL-STD-1553B (Avionics Variant)
description: Military standard avionics bus with redundant twisted-pair wiring, deterministic timing, and robust error detection for combat aircraft.

name: ACARS (Aircraft Communications Addressing)
description: Worldwide VHF/HF air-to-ground digital messaging system for automatic transmission of aircraft position, maintenance, and crew messages.

name: VDL Mode 2 (VHF Digital Link)
description: VHF digital link mode 2 providing air-to-air and air-to-ground data communication with automatic link establishment for modern aircraft.

name: VDL Mode 3
description: Legacy VDL mode 3 providing air-to-air communication between aircraft using single-channel simplex VHF transmission.

name: VDL Mode 4
description: High-capacity VDL mode 4 providing air-to-ground and air-to-air communication with greater bandwidth for future ATM systems.

name: ADS-B (Automatic Dependent Surveillance)
description: Aircraft automatic broadcast of position, altitude, velocity from GNSS providing surveillance for air traffic management and separation services.

name: ADS-C (Automatic Dependent Surveillance - Contract)
description: Air-to-ground data link for ADS-C providing aircraft position and status to air traffic control with conditional and demand reporting.

name: Mode S Transponder Data Link
description: Selective addressing transponder mode for discrete aircraft identification and interrogation enabling modern air traffic control surveillance.

name: ATN/IPS (Aeronautical Telecommunication Network)
description: Modern IP-based ATM communication network replacing legacy AFTN with packet-switched data, voice, and safety-critical services.

name: CPDLC (Controller-Pilot Data Link Communications)
description: Data link system replacing voice-based ATC clearances and instructions with digital messages for reduced pilot workload.

name: HFDL (High Frequency Data Link)
description: HF data link providing long-range oceanic aircraft communication for CPDLC and messaging over remote areas without VHF coverage.

name: Gatelink (Airport Gate Communication)
description: Aircraft-to-gate digital communication link for automated gate status, passenger boarding bridge control, and aircraft servicing coordination.

name: EFB Link (Electronic Flight Bag)
description: Wireless data link from airline operations to electronic flight bag tablets for weather, NOTAMs, flight plans, and crew information.

name: Airport Surface Detection Network
description: Ground surveillance radar and sensor network for detecting and tracking aircraft and vehicles on airport surface for safe ground movement.

name: AeroMACS (Airport WiMAX)
description: WiMAX-based mobile broadband network for airport operations providing connectivity to aircraft, vehicles, and personnel on movement areas.

name: L-DACS (L-band Digital Aeronautical Communications)
description: L-band spectrum aeronautical communication system providing high-capacity air-to-ground link as long-term VHF replacement.

name: SwiftBroadband (Inmarsat Aviation)
description: Inmarsat satellite-based broadband service for aircraft providing connectivity for voice, data, and inflight entertainment globally.

name: Ku/Ka Aviation Broadband
description: Ku and Ka-band satellite services for aircraft providing high-speed internet and streaming content with multiple providers.

name: FANS (Future Air Navigation System)
description: Integrated avionics system combining CPDLC and automatic dependent surveillance contract for oceanic and remote area navigation.

name: TIS-B (Traffic Information Service - Broadcast)
description: Ground-to-air broadcast service providing traffic information to equipage-limited aircraft on ADS-B-in capability.

name: FIS-B (Flight Information Service - Broadcast)
description: Ground-to-air broadcast of meteorological and aeronautical information to equipped aircraft without requiring two-way communication.

name: Leaky Feeder (Radiating Cable)
description: Radiating coaxial cable system providing VHF/UHF communication coverage in underground mines with inherent propagation mitigation.

name: Through-The-Earth (TTE) Communication
description: Ultra-low frequency ground-penetrating communication for trapped miner rescue and emergency signaling through rock strata.

name: PED (Post-Explosion Device)
description: Emergency communication device for signaling surface after underground blast explosion for immediate rescue coordination.

name: Mine WiFi (Ruggedized 802.11)
description: Industrial-grade WiFi mesh network with intrinsic safety rating for IP connectivity in underground mining operations.

name: Mine Mesh Network
description: Self-healing mesh topology for underground mining providing redundant paths and automatic rerouting around coverage dead zones.

name: RFID Mine Tracking
description: RFID-based personnel and equipment tracking system for real-time location awareness in underground mining environment.

name: UWB Mine Positioning
description: Ultra-wideband positioning system for high-accuracy location tracking through mine walls and in GPS-denied underground areas.

name: Cap Lamp Communication
description: Short-range communication system integrated into miner cap lamps for voice and text messaging with dead battery backup signaling.

name: Strata Communication System
description: Specialized mining communication platform combining radio, WiFi, and sensor networks for integrated operational and safety messaging.

name: Mine Fiber Backbone
description: Optical fiber backbone infrastructure for high-speed data transport connecting surface operations with underground control centers.

name: Mine Ethernet (Ruggedized)
description: Industrial Ethernet with intrinsic safety certification for networked mining equipment and sensor integration.

name: Seismic Sensor Network (Mine)
description: Distributed seismic monitoring sensors for ground stability assessment and rockburst prediction in deep underground mines.

name: Ventilation Monitoring Network
description: Sensor network monitoring mine ventilation parameters for safety including airflow direction, velocity, and integrity.

name: Gas Detection Sensor Network
description: Continuous gas detection sensors for methane, CO, and other hazardous gases with centralized monitoring and alarm distribution.

name: Mine Emergency Communication
description: Dedicated emergency communication system with guaranteed coverage and priority access for rescue coordination in mining disasters.

name: GSM-R (Global System for Mobile - Railway)
description: Dedicated GSM variant for railway providing voice and data communication for train operations with priority and pre-emption.

name: FRMCS (Future Railway Mobile Communication System)
description: Next-generation 5G-based railway communication system replacing GSM-R with enhanced bandwidth and mission-critical services.

name: ETCS (European Train Control System)
description: European standard automatic train protection and control system with continuous communication between train and trackside.

name: CBTC (Communications-Based Train Control)
description: Modern train control system using continuous radio communication for automatic train protection in urban transit systems.

name: PTC (Positive Train Control)
description: North American automatic train protection mandate using GPS and radio for collision avoidance and speed enforcement.

name: Balise Telegram (Eurobalise)
description: Trackside inductive transponder system transmitting safety-critical data to passing trains for ETCS integration.

name: Loop Cable Communication
description: Continuous inductive loop signal transmission for train detection and positive identification along railway track.

name: Axle Counter Network
description: Train detection network using wheel sensor counts for track occupancy determination and signaling logic.

name: Train Communication Network (TCN)
description: Distributed communication network within train for interconnecting on-board systems and transmitting to wayside.

name: MVB (Multifunction Vehicle Bus)
description: Real-time Ethernet-like bus for train-mounted equipment using master-slave protocol for deterministic communication.

name: WTB (Wire Train Bus)
description: High-speed Ethernet train backbone for real-time multimedia and safety-critical system interconnection.

name: ETB (Ethernet Train Backbone)
description: Distributed Ethernet-based backbone for high-bandwidth applications like surveillance, infotainment, and future autonomous trains.

name: TRDP (Train Real-time Data Protocol)
description: Real-time data protocol for TCN providing deterministic multicast over Ethernet for safety and comfort applications.

name: Trackside Network
description: Distributed fiber backbone connecting lineside equipment, signals, switches, and monitoring systems along track.

name: Station Network (Passenger Info)
description: IP-based network for passenger information systems, announcements, and station operations control.

name: TETRA Railway
description: TETRA digital radio system for railway operations providing encrypted voice and data with dedicated spectrum.

name: DMR Railway
description: Digital Mobile Radio system for railway providing dual-slot time-division voice and data on commercial spectrum.

name: Rail IoT Sensor Network
description: Distributed IoT sensors for track monitoring, bearing temperature, wheel flat detection, and predictive maintenance.

name: Traction Power SCADA
description: SCADA system for electrical traction power distribution monitoring and control along railway lines.

name: Signaling Interlocking Network
description: Distributed interlocking control network connecting signal heads, switches, and track circuits for safe train movement.

name: SDI (Serial Digital Interface)
description: Industry standard serial digital video interface transmitting 270 Mbps uncompressed SD video with embedded audio.

name: HD-SDI (SMPTE 292M)
description: High-definition serial digital interface at 1.485 Gbps for professional HD video production and distribution.

name: 3G-SDI (SMPTE 424M)
description: 3 Gbps serial digital interface supporting 1080p high frame rate and future HD formats with single coaxial connection.

name: 12G-SDI (SMPTE 2082)
description: Ultra-high-definition serial digital interface at 12 Gbps for 4K and 8K video production on single coax cable.

name: SMPTE 2110 (Professional Media over IP)
description: IP-based professional media transport standard separating audio, video, and metadata streams for broadcast infrastructure.

name: SMPTE 2022 (Video over IP)
description: IP video transport standard with FEC and seamless redundancy for reliable broadcast and streaming delivery.

name: NDI (Network Device Interface)
description: IP video transport standard for broadcasting uncompressed or lightly compressed video across standard networks.

name: Dante Audio Network
description: Professional audio networking protocol over standard Ethernet providing ultra-low-latency synchronized audio transport.

name: AES67 (Audio over IP)
description: Standard audio over IP format providing interoperability between professional audio networks using RTP transport.

name: AVB/TSN (Audio Video Bridging)
description: Time-sensitive networking standard with deterministic latency for synchronized audio/video delivery in professional environments.

name: MADI (Multichannel Audio Digital Interface)
description: Point-to-point digital audio interface carrying up to 64 channels of uncompressed audio on single optical or coax link.

name: AES3 (Digital Audio)
description: Standard professional digital audio interface carrying 2 channels on balanced XLR or unbalanced coax connection.

name: CoaXPress (High-speed Camera)
description: Coaxial high-speed camera interface supporting 10.625 Gbps for uncompressed video from industrial and broadcast cameras.

name: Camera Link
description: High-speed parallel digital camera interface standard providing 2.04 Gbps over multi-coax cables.

name: GigE Vision
description: GigE Ethernet-based camera interface standard for industrial vision and broadcast over standard IP networks.

name: GenICam
description: Generic camera interface standard providing unified control protocol for various camera transport layers.

name: FIX Protocol Network
description: Financial Information eXchange protocol for real-time trading order routing and execution with standardized message format.

name: FAST Protocol (Financial Information Exchange)
description: Fast Automated Standardized Trade-reporting protocol providing high-speed market data and execution messaging.

name: Kernel Bypass Network (DPDK)
description: User-space packet processing framework bypassing OS kernel for ultra-low-latency trading applications.

name: FPGA Trading Link
description: Hardware-accelerated FPGA-based trading system for minimal-latency order processing and execution.

name: Solarflare OpenOnload
description: Low-latency networking stack with hardware acceleration for trading and financial data applications.

name: ExaBlaze Trading
description: Ultra-low-latency trading infrastructure with hardware timestamping and optimized packet processing.

name: Co-location Cross-Connect
description: Direct physical cross-connections between systems at data center co-location for minimal-latency trading.

name: Market Data Multicast
description: Multicast distribution of real-time market data to multiple trading systems with redundant feeds.

name: Order Gateway Network
description: High-performance routing layer for order submission to exchanges with load balancing and failover.

name: Dark Pool Private Network
description: Private trading venue network for alternative execution with direct participant connections.

name: SWIFT Network (Banking)
description: Society for Worldwide Interbank Financial Telecommunication network for secure global financial message exchange.

name: FpML Network (Financial Products)
description: Financial products Markup Language standard for derivatives and structured products information exchange.

name: Nuclear I&C Network (IEC 61513)
description: Instrumentation and control network for nuclear reactors meeting IEC 61513 deterministic safety requirements with radiation hardening.

name: Safety System Network (Class 1E)
description: IEEE 623 Class 1E safety-related network for critical reactor protection functions with redundancy and independence.

name: NERVA Protocol
description: Nuclear Emergency Response Voice and data Architecture protocol for coordinated emergency communications at nuclear facilities.

name: Qualified Fiber Optic (Nuclear)
description: Radiation-qualified optical fiber communication meeting nuclear facility requirements for isolation and EMI immunity.

name: Radiation Hardened Ethernet
description: Radiation-hardened networking hardware and components for long-term operation in high-radiation nuclear environments.

name: Nuclear SCADA
description: SCADA system for nuclear facility operations monitoring and non-safety systems control with cybersecurity hardening.

name: Containment Penetration Link
description: Qualified communication link through reactor containment walls for isolated sensor and control signals.

name: Remote Monitoring Network (IAEA)
description: International monitoring network under IAEA safeguards for nuclear facility remote verification and compliance.

name: Spent Fuel Monitoring
description: Real-time monitoring network for spent fuel pools providing temperature, water level, and radiation data.

name: Health Physics Network
description: Radiation monitoring sensor network for occupational and environmental dosimetry throughout nuclear facility.

name: Emergency Response Network (Nuclear)
description: Dedicated emergency communication system for coordinating on-site and off-site response to nuclear incidents.

name: Seismic Monitoring Network (Nuclear)
description: High-sensitivity seismic monitoring array for earthquake detection and nuclear facility structural integrity assessment.

name: P25 (Project 25)
description: Digital radio standard for emergency services providing encrypted voice and data on dedicated spectrum.

name: TETRA (Terrestrial Trunked Radio)
description: European digital trunked radio system for emergency services with guaranteed bandwidth and priority access.

name: DMR (Digital Mobile Radio)
description: Digital mobile radio standard for emergency services using dual-slot time-division for two voice channels.

name: NXDN
description: Next Generation Digital Radio system for emergency services with efficient spectrum usage and digital voice.

name: FirstNet (Band 14 LTE)
description: Nationwide public safety LTE broadband network on dedicated Band 14 spectrum for emergency services.

name: MCPTT (Mission Critical PTT)
description: Mission-critical push-to-talk application on LTE/5G providing guaranteed service for emergency communications.

name: MCVideo
description: Mission-critical video application on LTE/5G for real-time situational awareness and incident documentation.

name: MCData
description: Mission-critical data application on LTE/5G for secure information exchange and command-and-control.

name: APCO P25 Phase 2
description: Enhanced P25 standard with higher spectral efficiency and improved voice quality for emergency services.

name: ALERT (Automated Local Evaluation Real-Time)
description: Automated emergency alert system for hazard detection and community notification.

name: CAP (Common Alerting Protocol)
description: Standardized XML format for emergency alerts enabling interoperability across multiple alerting systems.

name: IPAWS (Integrated Public Alert)
description: Federal integrated alert system distributing emergency messages through multiple channels and carriers.

name: Emergency Alert System (EAS)
description: National emergency alert system for broadcast TV/radio disseminating emergency information to the public.

name: Amber Alert Network
description: Child abduction alert system integrated with EAS, wireless alerts, and highway signs.

name: 911 NG Network (Next Generation 911)
description: IP-based next-generation 911 system supporting text, video, and location data for enhanced emergency response.

name: Ultrasonic Data Link
description: Data transmission using ultrasonic frequencies above human hearing for side-channel or proximity communication.

name: Acoustic Mesh Network
description: Multi-hop mesh topology using acoustic modulation for underwater or obstructed environments.

name: Sonic LAN
description: Local-area network using audible-range acoustic signals for communication over air or through structures.

name: Infrasound Sensor Network
description: Detection network for low-frequency sound waves used in meteorological monitoring and disaster early warning.

name: Parametric Speaker Network
description: Directional sound projection using parametric speakers for targeted acoustic communication and data transmission.

name: Audio Watermark Data Channel
description: Embedding data in audio signals using inaudible watermarks for covert communication or side-channel.

name: Chirp Spread Spectrum Audio
description: Spread spectrum modulation using chirp signals for robust acoustic communication in noisy environments.

name: Near-Ultrasonic Communication
description: Data transmission using near-ultrasonic frequencies at edge of human hearing for privacy and range.

name: Acoustic Fence Network
description: Distributed acoustic sensors creating perimeter detection using ground vibration and acoustic signatures.

name: Vibration Communication (through solids)
description: Transmission of data through solid materials using mechanical vibrations for secure or covert channels.

name: MAVLink (Micro Air Vehicle Link)
description: Open-source UAV control protocol for communication between ground control station and aerial vehicles.

name: DroneID
description: Standard for remote unmanned aircraft identification for traffic awareness and regulatory compliance.

name: RemoteID (FAA)
description: FAA-mandated remote identification broadcast of drone identity, location, and operator information.

name: STANAG 4586 (UAV Control)
description: NATO standard for control of unmanned air vehicles with standardized interfaces and protocols.

name: STANAG 4609 (UAV Video)
description: NATO standard for motion imagery transmission from unmanned aircraft to ground stations.

name: C2 Link (Command and Control)
description: Military command and control data link for UAV operation with encrypted communications.

name: Mesh UAV Swarm Network
description: Multi-hop mesh topology for coordinated autonomous UAV swarms with distributed control.

name: UAV Satellite Link
description: Satellite communication link for beyond-line-of-sight UAV operations with global coverage.

name: FPV Video Link (5.8 GHz)
description: First-person-view analog video transmission on 5.8 GHz ISM band for drone piloting.

name: DJI OcuSync
description: DJI proprietary video transmission system using 2.4/5.8 GHz for consumer and professional drones.

name: Crossfire/ELRS (ExpressLRS)
description: Low-latency radio control protocol for FPV racing and freestyle drones with long-range capability.

name: UAV Cellular (LTE/5G)
description: Cellular LTE or 5G connectivity for UAVs providing beyond-visual-line-of-sight operation.

name: UTM Network (UAS Traffic Management)
description: Airspace management system coordinating multiple UAVs for deconfliction and safe operations.

name: Counter-UAS Detection Network
description: Detection and tracking network for unauthorized UAVs using RF, radar, and optical sensors.

name: UAV Relay Network
description: Multi-hop relay network using UAVs as airborne relays to extend ground communication range.
